title: Beyond Helping Others: The Unexpected Gains from Volunteering
title_zh: 超越助人：从志愿服务中获得的意外收获
intro_zh: 本文约1000词，探讨做志愿者的多重收获，包括个人成长、社交拓展、职业发展和心理健康，并引用调查数据支持观点。

paragraphs.en: Many people think volunteering is simply about giving time to help others. However, research and personal stories show that volunteering brings unexpected benefits to the volunteers themselves. From building new skills to improving mental health, the gains are often more profound than the help provided.
paragraphs.zh: 许多人认为志愿服务仅仅是付出时间帮助他人。然而，研究和个人经历表明，志愿服务为志愿者本人带来了意想不到的好处。从培养新技能到改善心理健康，这些收获往往比所提供的帮助更加深远。

paragraphs.en: First, volunteering helps people develop practical skills. For example, a college student who tutors children in math may improve her own communication and leadership abilities. A 2021 survey by the Corporation for National and Community Service found that 76% of volunteers said they gained new skills through their service. These abilities can be valuable in future career paths.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，志愿服务帮助人们培养实用技能。例如，一名辅导儿童数学的大学生可能提高自己的沟通和领导能力。美国国家与社区服务公司2021年的一项调查发现，76%的志愿者表示他们通过服务获得了新技能。这些能力在未来的职业道路上可能很有价值。

paragraphs.en: Second, volunteering creates social connections. When people work together for a common cause, they often form strong bonds. A study from the University of Michigan showed that volunteers have larger social networks and higher levels of support. For international students or newcomers to a city, volunteering can be a great way to meet people and feel a sense of belonging.
paragraphs.zh: 其次，志愿服务创造社交联系。当人们为了共同的目标一起工作时，往往会形成牢固的纽带。密歇根大学的一项研究表明，志愿者拥有更大的社交网络和更高水平的支持。对于国际学生或刚到一个城市的人来说，志愿服务是结识朋友、获得归属感的好方法。

paragraphs.en: Third, volunteering boosts mental health. Helping others can reduce stress and increase happiness. A longitudinal study by the London School of Economics found that people who volunteered reported higher life satisfaction than those who did not. The act of giving releases endorphins, creating a natural high often called "helper's high".
paragraphs.zh: 第三，志愿服务促进心理健康。帮助他人可以减轻压力、增加幸福感。伦敦政治经济学院的一项纵向研究发现，参与志愿服务的人比不参与的人生活满意度更高。给予的行为会释放内啡肽，产生一种被称为“助人者快感”的自然愉悦感。

paragraphs.en: Fourth, volunteering offers a sense of purpose. Many people struggle to find meaning in their daily routines. By contributing to something larger than themselves, volunteers often feel a stronger sense of purpose. For example, a retired teacher who volunteers at a local library may feel valued and needed again.
paragraphs.zh: 第四，志愿服务提供目标感。许多人在日常生活中难以找到意义。通过为比自己更大的事业做贡献，志愿者常常感受到更强的目标感。例如，一位退休教师在当地图书馆做志愿者，可能会再次感到被重视和被需要。

paragraphs.en: Fifth, volunteering can improve career prospects. Employers often look for candidates with vital soft skills like teamwork and problem-solving. Volunteering provides real-world experience that can be listed on a resume. A 2022 LinkedIn survey found that 41% of hiring managers considered volunteer work as valuable as paid work when evaluating candidates.
paragraphs.zh: 第五，志愿服务可以提升职业前景。雇主通常寻找具备团队合作和解决问题等重要软技能的候选人。志愿服务提供了可以写在简历上的真实经验。2022年领英的一项调查发现，41%的招聘经理在评估候选人时认为志愿工作和有偿工作同等有价值。

paragraphs.en: Lastly, volunteering fosters personal growth. Volunteers often step out of their comfort zones and face new challenges. This builds resilience and self-confidence. A 2019 study in the Journal of Happiness Studies found that volunteers reported higher levels of personal growth compared to non-volunteers.
paragraphs.zh: 最后，志愿服务促进个人成长。志愿者常常走出舒适区，面对新的挑战。这培养了他们韧性和自信心。2019年《幸福研究杂志》的一项研究发现，与非志愿者相比，志愿者报告了更高水平的个人成长。

paragraphs.en: In conclusion, volunteering is not just a one-way street of giving. It brings multiple benefits to the volunteer, from skill development and social connections to mental well-being and career advancement. So why not start today? Find a cause you care about and experience the transformative power of helping others.
paragraphs.zh: 总之，志愿服务并非单向的付出。它为志愿者带来了多重好处，从技能发展和社交联系到心理健康和职业发展。那么，何不从今天开始？找一个你关心的事业，体验帮助他人的变革性力量。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Volunteering is only beneficial for the recipients.
Volunteering offers unexpected gains to volunteers.
Volunteering is a waste of time.
questions.explain: 主旨题。文章开篇即点明志愿服务为志愿者带来意想不到的好处，后续段落分别从技能、社交、心理、目标、职业、个人成长等方面阐述。选项B正确。

questions.q: According to the 2021 survey mentioned in paragraph 2, what percentage of volunteers gained new skills?
questions.explain: 细节题。第二段明确提到“a 2021 survey ... found that 76% of volunteers said they gained new skills”。选项B正确。

questions.q: What does the word 'endorphins' in paragraph 4 most likely mean?
options: Natural substances that make you feel good
questions.explain: 词义题。第四段提到“releases endorphins, creating a natural high”，说明内啡肽是能带来愉悦感的天然物质。选项C正确。

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a benefit of volunteering in the passage?
questions.explain: 细节题。文章提到了心理健康（第三段）、沟通技能（第二段）、社交网络（第三段），但未提及赚钱。选项B不是文章提到的益处。
